name: Headless commerce: Vendure + Next.js
description: Headless e-commerce template with Vendure (GraphQL, Stripe, PostgreSQL, Redis) and a Next.js storefront, Docker/Podman deploys, and Caddy for production HTTPS.
longDescription: A commerce reference implementation pairing Vendure on Node.js/TypeScript with a Next.js storefront. The commerce layer exposes GraphQL Shop and Admin APIs, supports Stripe (cards, MB WAY, Multibanco and related local payment methods where configured), EU VAT/B2B-oriented custom fields, and S3/MinIO-backed assets. The storefront uses SSR, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, and Zustand. Deployment is container-first with compose files, Caddy as reverse proxy in production, and a Windows manage.ps1 console for common lifecycle tasks.
features: Multi-channel commerce core with orders, products, and admin API
Next.js storefront with theme contract and Vendure GraphQL client
Stripe payment flows and asset storage integration
Caddy configuration for automatic HTTPS where a domain is available
manage.ps1-driven workflows for build, deploy, and packaging
highlights: End-to-end headless pattern from catalog to checkout integration
Operational scripts that mirror how Windows-based operators run containers
Documentation for observability and deployment variants shipped with the project
challenges: Headless commerce requires tight alignment between storefront UX, payment webhooks, and long-lived database migrations across Vendure upgrades.
solutions: Keep vendure and storefront in one workspace with shared tooling, health-checked compose services, and documented environment separation for dev vs prod.
security: Secrets and keys only via environment configuration
Reverse proxy and TLS termination documented for production shapes
Stripe and webhook configuration treated as sensitive operations
